title: artemisia pubescens var. pumila
intro: Artemisia pubescens var. pumila, also known as dwarf wormwood, is a low-growing shrub native to the mountainous regions of China and the Himalayas. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and antispasmodic qualities, which make it effective in treating digestive issues and relieving pain. The health benefits of dwarf wormwood include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Dwarf wormwood can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
